explanation: This command represents a typical use of rundll32 with an ordinal call. It is not obfuscated and would be detected by the Sigma rule.
Adding leading zeros to the ordinal number is a common obfuscation technique. This command is a safe mutation that may bypass the Sigma rule.
Using a larger number that wraps around in 16-bit space (65536 + 24 = 65560) is another obfuscation technique. This command is a safe mutation that may bypass the Sigma rule.
Adding a space between the comma and the ordinal number is a safe mutation that may bypass the Sigma rule.
Using hexadecimal escape sequences to represent the ordinal number is a safe mutation that may bypass the Sigma rule.
Using command substitution to dynamically generate the ordinal number is a safe mutation that may bypass the Sigma rule.
